title: Conjecture 2 (Conjecture 6.1 [1])
statement_text: For any graph $G$, the collection $\{\mathcal{C}_{k}(G)\}_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$ uniquely determines $G$.
context_text: Asgarli, Krehbiel, Levinson and Russell conjectured that the collection of all $k$-colouring graphs of $G$, or equivalently the collection of all generalised chromatic polynomials $\{\pi^{(H)}_{G}(k)\}_{H\in\mathcal{G}}$, forms a complete graph invariant. This is the weaker of their two conjectures on the topic.
paper_contribution: Confirmed by proving Theorem 4: for any graph $G$ on $n$ vertices and any $k > 5n^2$, the single colouring graph $\mathcal{C}_k(G)$ already uniquely determines $G$ up to isomorphism.

title: Conjecture 3 (Conjecture 6.2 [1])
statement_text: There exists some function $f:\mathcal{G}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that for any graph $G$, the collection $\{\mathcal{C}_{k}(G)\}^{f(G)}_{k=1}$ uniquely determines $G$.
context_text: This is the stronger companion conjecture to Conjecture 6.1 of Asgarli et al., asserting that finitely many colouring graphs (depending on $G$) suffice to reconstruct $G$, rather than the full infinite collection.
paper_contribution: Confirmed as a consequence of Theorem 4: taking $f(G) = 5n^2 + 1$ where $n = |V(G)|$, a single colouring graph $\mathcal{C}_k(G)$ with $k > 5n^2$ suffices to uniquely determine $G$.

title: Problem 11
statement_text: For which graphs $H$ does there exist an $H^{\prime}$ such that $\pi_{G}^{(H^{\prime})}$ determines $\pi_{G}^{(H)}$ for every graph $G$?
context_text: Asgarli et al. conjectured (their Conjecture 5.2) that $\pi_{G}^{(K_{2})}$ determines the chromatic polynomial $\pi_{G}^{(K_{1})}$, i.e., that equal edge-count polynomials of colouring graphs imply equal chromatic polynomials. This broader problem asks for which pairs $(H, H')$ such a determination holds, motivated by Remark 10 in the paper.

title: Problem 12
statement_text: Is there a finite set of graphs $H_{1},\dotsc,H_{t}$ such that, for almost every $G\in\mathcal{G}(n,\frac{1}{2})$, if $\pi_{G^{\prime}}^{(H_{i})}=\pi_{G}^{(H_{i})}$ for all $i$ then $G^{\prime}\cong G$?
context_text: Theorem 5 of the paper shows that no finite family of generalised chromatic polynomials can distinguish all graphs; this problem asks whether the situation is better for typical (Erdős–Rényi random) graphs. It is posed as a weakening of the Bollobás–Pebody–Riordan conjecture that almost every graph is determined by its chromatic polynomial.
